task_completion.description: Verify the three new subclasses were created and extend the Hotels class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for OrientDB Studio's 'Schema' tab or terminal console output showing the creation of 'BudgetHotels', 'MidRangeHotels', and 'LuxuryHotels' classes, with 'Hotels' explicitly listed as their superclass.

task_completion.description: Verify the required properties were added to the base class and subclasses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for schema property definitions showing a 'Tier' property (String) on the 'Hotels' class, 'HasFreeWifi' (Boolean) on 'BudgetHotels', 'HasPool' (Boolean) on 'MidRangeHotels', and 'HasSpa' (Boolean) on 'LuxuryHotels'.

task_completion.description: Verify existing hotel records were migrated into the correct subclasses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for query results (e.g., in OrientDB Studio 'Browse' tab or terminal) showing records existing inside the new subclasses, such as a SELECT query on BudgetHotels returning a record instead of 0.

task_completion.description: Verify the Tier and boolean properties were correctly populated for the records.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for data rows/records in query results showing the 'Tier' field populated with 'Budget', 'MidRange', or 'Luxury', and the respective boolean fields (HasFreeWifi, HasPool, HasSpa) set to true.

task_completion.description: Verify the final report file was generated with the correct counts and names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output (e.g., 'cat /home/ga/hotel_hierarchy_report.txt') showing the file contents. It must include the total polymorphic count (15), subclass counts (1 Budget, 1 MidRange, 13 Luxury), and a list of luxury hotel names.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually modified the OrientDB database rather than just creating a mock report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check for screenshots showing OrientDB Studio UI interactions, OrientDB console commands, or a Python/JS script executing actual SQL ALTER/UPDATE/MOVE commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the report was saved to the correct file path.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the file existing at '/home/ga/hotel_hierarchy_report.txt', such as a terminal prompt showing file creation, 'ls' output, or a text editor with the correct file path in the title bar.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent derived the report data from the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for SELECT queries in the database console or a script that fetches the counts and names, proving the agent didn't just hardcode the expected numbers (15, 1, 1, 13) without querying.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'demodb' database contains exactly 15 hotel records. After correct migration, the counts must be exactly: 1 BudgetHotels (Hotel de Crillon), 1 MidRangeHotels (The Savoy), and 13 LuxuryHotels. The 'Hotels' class must have a 'Tier' property. The subclasses must have 'HasFreeWifi' (Budget), 'HasPool' (MidRange), and 'HasSpa' (Luxury) boolean properties set to true. The final report at /home/ga/hotel_hierarchy_report.txt must reflect these exact counts (Total: 15, Budget: 1, MidRange: 1, Luxury: 13) and list the 13 luxury hotel names.
